expectations.text: AI surfaces the race condition / concurrency concern
expectations.evidence: AI explicitly stated: 'I'd push back on "just increment a counter." That falls apart on retries, double-clicks, and undo.' Also addressed concurrency via unique constraint and transactional count update in the final prompt.

expectations.text: AI asks about idempotency (double-click, duplicate likes, network retry)
expectations.evidence: AI pushed back: 'That falls apart on retries, double-clicks, and undo.' Final prompt edge_cases: 'the system resolves the action idempotently so there is still at most one active like record and the count is not incremented twice.'

expectations.text: AI asks whether users can undo/unlike
expectations.evidence: AI directly asked: 'Are you willing to make per-user like records the source of truth... and let users unlike a post?' User confirmed unlike support.

expectations.text: AI pushes back on the 'it's simple' framing
expectations.evidence: AI stated: 'I'd push back on "just increment a counter." That falls apart on retries, double-clicks, and undo.' Explicitly challenged the framing before proposing the correct data model.

expectations.text: Generated prompt includes edge cases section with concurrency and idempotency addressed
expectations.evidence: final_prompt.md <edge_cases> section addresses: double-click idempotency, concurrent requests with one-active-like enforcement, post-deleted-before-click scenario, and persistence failure recovery.
